factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with increases in single-family housing starts but a decline in multi-family customer segment.

evidence.text: Core organic sales decreased net sales by 3.8%, primarily due to a continued downward trend in the multi-family customer segment partially offset by increases in the single-family and repair and remodel and other customer segments.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: The company is experiencing margin normalization, particularly in multi-family operations, but has seen some price increases due to commodity inflation.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage decreased...due to margin normalization, particularly in our multi-family operations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting with increases in lumber and lumber sheet goods and specialty building products, while manufactured products are declining.

evidence.text: Lumber & lumber sheet goods: 12.7% increase; Specialty building products & services: 2.3% increase; Manufactured products: 18.5% decrease.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to a decline in multi-family customer demand.

evidence.text: Core organic sales decreased net sales by 3.8%, primarily due to a continued downward trend in the multi-family customer segment.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed with increases in single-family housing starts but a decline in multi-family volumes.

evidence.text: Actual U.S. single-family starts increased 7.4%... while multi-family continues to trend downward.

factors.summary: The single-family housing market is growing, while the multi-family market is declining. Repair and remodel markets are forecasted to have slight growth.

evidence.text: Single-family starts increased 7.4%... Repair and remodel end market to increase approximately 1.2% in 2024.

factors.summary: Commodity prices are causing margin pressure, particularly in lumber and sheet goods, but the company is trying to offset through pricing.

evidence.text: Commodity price inflation increased net sales by 0.3%, while gross margin percentage decreased due to margin normalization.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with a decrease in variable compensation expenses.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses decreased... primarily due to lower variable compensation.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to margin normalization and lower gross margins.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage decreased to 32.8%... a 2.4% decrease from 35.2% in the second quarter of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering savings, particularly in variable compensation.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses decreased by $44.7 million... primarily due to lower variable compensation.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident through lower selling, general, and administrative expenses as a percentage of net sales.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses... decreased to 21.8%, down from 22.5% in the second quarter of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2024 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

factors.summary: The supply chain is facing disruptions, particularly in lumber prices and supply shortages.

evidence.text: The cyclical nature of the homebuilding industry;... disruptions in our supply chain.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is stable, with no significant changes mentioned.

evidence.text: No specific mention of capacity utilization changes.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed, with the company carrying more inventory during peak seasons.

evidence.text: Working capital levels typically increase in the first and second quarters... following the peak building and construction season.

factors.summary: Management is cautious about future guidance due to economic conditions and market trends.

evidence.text: The Company undertakes no obligation to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statements.

factors.summary: Management is confident in long-term trends but cautious about short-term challenges.

evidence.text: Well-positioned to take advantage of favorable long-term industry trends... challenges from higher interest rates and inflation.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include higher interest rates, inflation, and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Higher interest rates and inflation have impacted demand and affordability.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on market expansion through acquisitions and organic growth.

evidence.text: Strategically increase our market share, both organically and through acquisitions.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include housing starts, lumber prices, economic conditions, and interest rates.

evidence.text: Long-term outlook for the housing industry is positive... challenges from higher interest rates and inflation.

factors.summary: Catalysts include favorable long-term industry trends and successful integration of recent acquisitions.

evidence.text: Well-positioned to take advantage of favorable long-term industry trends... through acquisitions.

factors.summary: The company aims to increase market share through acquisitions and organic growth.

evidence.text: Strategically increase our market share, both organically and through acquisitions.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics include challenges from higher interest rates and inflation, impacting demand and affordability.

evidence.text: Higher interest rates and inflation have impacted demand and affordability for consumers, investors and builders.

factors.summary: The company differentiates through an integrated solution offering manufacturing, supply, and installation.

evidence.text: Provide an integrated solution... manufacturing, supply and installation of a full range of structural and related building products.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind due to higher interest rates and inflation, affecting demand and affordability.

evidence.text: Higher interest rates and inflation have impacted demand and affordability for consumers, investors and builders.

factors.summary: Higher interest rates are negatively impacting demand and affordability, acting as a headwind.

evidence.text: Higher interest rates and inflation have impacted demand and affordability.

factors.summary: Construction activity is mixed with increases in single-family housing starts but a decline in multi-family spending.

evidence.text: Actual U.S. single-family starts increased 7.4%... while multi-family continues to trend downward.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.

evidence.text: Revolving Facility is primarily used for working capital, general corporate purposes and funding capital expenditures.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is manageable with net excess borrowing availability of $1.6 billion.

evidence.text: Net excess borrowing availability on revolving facility $1,634.7 million.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are active with share repurchases and dividend considerations.

evidence.text: Repurchased 5.9 million shares... authorized a new repurchase plan of up to $1.0 billion.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is stable with a decrease in cash provided by operating activities.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities decreased... largely the result of lower net income.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions are active with recent acquisitions contributing to net sales growth.

evidence.text: Completed acquisitions... for an aggregate purchase price of approximately $132.9 million.
